questions.question: How should I apply the ad-creative skill to the campaign?

options.label: Copy-only refresh (Recommended)
options.description: Generate fresh headline/sub/CTA/primary-text variants per placement using the ad-creative copy framework (hook angles, benefit-led, social proof, urgency). Drop the new copy into the existing 8 layouts. Fastest path, keeps visual system.

options.label: Copy + visual redesign
options.description: Generate new copy AND rebuild each placement's composition from scratch with different visual strategies per variant (e.g. typographic hero, photographic hero, product-on-color, lifestyle crop). Bigger lift, more variation.

options.label: Full strategy rebuild
options.description: Treat campaign as new: 3-4 distinct ad-creative angles (e.g. morning ritual, afternoon slump, sundown aperitif, post-workout), each with its own copy + visual treatment across all 4 placements. ~12-16 creatives.

Answer questions?

content:
Heads up before I dive in — the `ad-creative` skill installed here is a **catalogue stub**, not the full upstream workflow. Its scope is also narrower than what you're asking for: it's a **copy-iteration skill** (headlines, descriptions, primary text for paid social/search), not a visual redesign skill. The full workflow lives upstream at github.com/coreyhaines31/marketingskills and isn't bundled.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- A "redesign" using ad-creative would really mean: regenerate the **copy layer** (headline / sub / CTA / primary text variants) for each placement, then I rebuild the visual compositions around the new copy — keeping your GIMBER brand system, bottle fidelity, and no-overlap zones intact.
- The visual A/B framework (editorial-A vs lifestyle/promo-B) and the zone-based layout system stay as the chassis. What changes is the message strategy — currently every ad runs the same "Your natural kick" + "-20% summer bundle" message, which is a wasted A/B opportunity.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Two ways I can interpret "redesign with ad-creative":
